published.text:
### Section: Services intro paragraph
Added sentence:
If you already know you probably need expert help and want to evaluate fit and first-project scope before looking at pricing, start with our AI automation consultant guide for small business.

Placement notes:
- Inserted immediately after the existing link to "what an AI automation consultant actually does"
- Inserted immediately before the existing pricing sentence/link
- Linked page: `/ai-automation-consultant-small-business`
- Anchor text: `AI automation consultant guide for small business`

edits.description: This was a bounded homepage indexing-support refresh, not a new page ship.
The copy uses buyer-language rather than SEO jargon: "expert help," "fit," and "first-project scope" reflect the real decision stage before pricing.
The sentence works as editorial routing, not navigation chrome. It reads like the next practical question after the role explainer and before the pricing page.
The goal is stronger in-copy homepage-origin crawl context for the still-discovered-not-indexed consultant guide.
The sequence matters: role understanding -> consultant-fit evaluation -> pricing.
